prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: The song "Hallelujah" by Leonard Cohen is one piece that deeply moves me. The emotional depth conveyed through its poetic lyrics and caliginous melody is simply profound. Cohen's plaintive yet eloquent rendering of love, life, and spirituality is not just enchanting, but also deeply resonant. Each time I listen, it feels as though Cohen is exploring my own intimate experiences and sentiments, turning them into universal truths that resonate with all listeners. This unique ability to bridge the personal and universal is what makes "Hallelujah" so moving and timeless.
